BotJob.description: Processing view returned when a job is claimed.
BotJob.title: BotJob

JobDetail.description: Full read view of a single job, including its ``payload``.
JobDetail.title: JobDetail

JobKindCounts.description: Queue funnel for a single job kind: depth (``total``/``open``) and the status breakdown.
JobKindCounts.title: JobKindCounts

JobListItem.description: List view of a job. Omits the ``payload`` -- fetch a single job by id for that.
JobListItem.title: JobListItem

JobPage.description: A page of jobs plus the total match count, for offset pagination.
JobPage.title: JobPage

JobQueueSummary.description:
Queue funnel: global depth (``total``/``open``) and status counts, plus a per-kind breakdown.

``open`` is outstanding work -- ``pending`` (including jobs delayed by retry backoff) plus
``claimed``. Terminal counts are historical totals (rows are retained). ``by_kind`` is sorted by
kind and scoped to whatever ``kind`` filter the summary was fetched with.
JobQueueSummary.title: JobQueueSummary

JobResponse.description: State view returned after completing or failing a job.
JobResponse.title: JobResponse

JobStatusCounts.description: Job counts for every status in the queue funnel, zero-filled (no silent gaps).
JobStatusCounts.title: JobStatusCounts

OperationPage.description: A page of operations plus the total match count, for offset pagination.
OperationPage.title: OperationPage

OperationResponse.description: Full read view of a single operation, including the two-phase ``plan``.
OperationResponse.title: OperationResponse

OperationSummary.description: List view of an operation. Omits the heavy ``plan`` -- fetch a single operation by id for that.
OperationSummary.title: OperationSummary

OperationalProfile.description: Operational view of a party. Intentionally excludes billing/finance data.
OperationalProfile.title: OperationalProfile

schema.title: Response Read Application Clients Api V1 Auth Clients Get

post.description:
Check whether an actor may perform an action on a target party (direct or delegated).

Combines the grant engine (*whether* the actor may run `action_key`) with the delegation set
derived from `PartyRelation` (*on whose behalf* -- own party plus `PARENT_OF` / `PAYS_FOR`
targets). An inactive actor is denied; an unknown actor returns 404.

example.detail: Owner already owns a command env of this kind in the guild

409.description: Owner already owns a command env of this kind in the guild

example.detail: Command env references an unknown guild, channel, or owner

422.description: Command env references an unknown guild, channel, or owner

get.description: List jobs by status / kind, newest first. List items omit the `payload`; read a job by id for it.

post.description:
Claim up to `limit` due jobs for processing.

**Delivery is at-least-once.** A claimed job whose worker dies is reclaimed once its lease
(5 minutes) expires and handed out again, so the same job can be delivered more than once.
Re-delivery is real, not hypothetical -- handlers **must be idempotent** and must not
trigger a duplicate side effect on a job they have already processed.

schema.title: Response Claim Jobs Endpoint Api V1 Bot Jobs Claim Post

get.description:
Queue funnel (depth + status counts, globally and per kind) for operator observability.

Passing `kind` scopes the whole summary -- top-level totals and the `by_kind` list -- to that
exact job kind.
Read a single job by id, including its `payload`.

example.detail: Job is not in a claimed state

409.description: Job is not in a claimed state

post.description:
Report a claimed job as failed.

With `retry` set and attempts remaining, the job is requeued with backoff; otherwise it is
dead-lettered to `failed`. A worker that crashes without reporting reaches the same outcome
automatically once its lease expires (see the at-least-once contract on `claim`).

example.detail: Job is not in a claimed state

409.description: Job is not in a claimed state

get.description:
List operations by subject / status / kind, newest first, for post-restart reconciliation.

All filters are optional and AND-combined; the subject is the pair
(`guild_id`, `subject_discord_id`). List items omit the `plan` -- read a single operation by id
for its full plan.
Read a single operation by id, including its two-phase `plan`.

put.description:
Register or update a Discord user.

Idempotent: re-registering an existing `discord_id` updates role/nick/active instead of
conflicting, so the bot can safely replay an onboarding event.

delete.description:
Deactivate (unlink) a Discord account without deleting it.

Sets `active` to false and clears `is_primary`, freeing the party's primary slot while keeping
the row for history. Re-linking via the `PUT` endpoint reactivates it.

put.description:
Link a Discord account to an existing party.

The party must already exist (the CRM owns party creation); linking to an unknown party returns
404. Promoting an account to primary demotes the party's current primary account in the same
transaction.

example.detail: Another primary account already exists for this party

409.description: Another primary account already exists for this party

delete.description: Remove a Discord user from a permission group.

put.description: Add a Discord user to a permission group. Idempotent on (user, group).

example.detail: Discord user not found or Permission group not found

404.description: Discord user not found or Permission group not found

get.description: Aggregate health across all dependencies and workers.
Aggregate health across all external dependencies.
Liveness probe: the process is up and serving requests.
Aggregate health across all background workers.
Health of a single background worker.
